quests.passage: The Amazon rainforest covers over 5.5 million square kilometers across nine countries in South America.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: Look for the number of countries.

quests.passage: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Octopuses have three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills, while the third pumps it to the rest of the body.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest coral reef system in the world, stretching over 2,300 kilometers along Australia's coast.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between 1503 and 1519. It now hangs in the Louvre Museum in Paris.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Water covers about 71 percent of the Earth's surface, but only 2.5 percent of it is freshwater.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth at 8,849 meters above sea level. It is located on the border of Nepal and Tibet.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The human brain weighs about 1.4 kilograms and uses approximately 20 percent of the body's total energy.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: Look for the percentage of energy.

quests.passage: Cheetahs are the fastest land animals, reaching speeds up to 112 kilometers per hour in short bursts.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert in the world, covering about 9.2 million square kilometers in North Africa.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Butterflies taste with their feet. They have sensors on their legs that help them identify plants for laying eggs.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The Dead Sea is nearly 10 times saltier than the ocean. Its high salt content makes it easy to float on the surface.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Thomas Edison held 1,093 patents. His most famous invention was the practical incandescent light bulb.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system with surface temperatures reaching 465 degrees Celsius.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: The planet is named in the passage.

quests.passage: Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump. Despite their size, they are excellent swimmers.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second. Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not. Botanically, a berry develops from a single flower with one ovary.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Japan has over 6,800 islands, but most people live on the four largest: Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: Look for the number of islands.

quests.passage: A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance. These birds get their pink color from the carotenoids in their diet.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: Look for the source of color.

quests.passage: The International Space Station orbits Earth at about 28,000 km/h. It completes one orbit every 90 minutes.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Sharks have been around for over 450 million years, making them older than dinosaurs and even trees.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Coffee is the second most traded commodity in the world after oil. Brazil is the largest producer.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
quests.hint: Coffee is second, what is first?

quests.passage: The Mariana Trench is the deepest part of the ocean, reaching a depth of nearly 11,000 meters.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day. Their diet of eucalyptus leaves provides very little energy.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Iceland is powered almost entirely by renewable energy. About 85% comes from geothermal and hydropower sources.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The human eye can distinguish about 10 million different colors. However, some animals can see ultraviolet light.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Penguins propose to their mates with a pebble. If the female accepts, they become partners for life.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The Nile is the longest river in the world at 6,650 kilometers. It flows through eleven countries in northeastern Africa.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: Giraffes only need 30 minutes of sleep per day. They usually sleep in short naps of a few minutes each.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?

quests.passage: The first email was sent in 1971 by Ray Tomlinson. He also chose the '@' symbol for email addresses.
quests.question: What conclusion can you draw from this text?
